response.justification: While the candidate demonstrates strong software engineering fundamentals and direct IDE experience from National Instruments, they lack the critical technical requirements. There is minimal Java experience beyond brief mentions and zero Kotlin expertise or evidence of deep JVM knowledge. Most critically, there is no Spring ecosystem or enterprise framework experience (Spring Boot, Hibernate, Quarkus, application servers), which are core to this role. The candidate's career has focused on full-stack web development and modern TypeScript/Node.js platforms, moving further away from the JVM ecosystem rather than developing deeper expertise in it.
strengths: Strong software engineering fundamentals with proven track record in senior/staff roles at tier-1 companies (Naver, Tencent, National Instruments)
Direct IDE development experience from LabWindows/CVI role at National Instruments, plus language tooling expertise through open-source frameworks (Trial, Fluent-Asserts)
Demonstrated ability to design and maintain complex systems with architectural consistency, including microservices handling millions of requests at Sociomantic
concerns: Minimal Java experience (brief mentions at Altom); zero Kotlin expertise or evidence of deep JVM knowledge
No Spring ecosystem or enterprise framework experience (Spring Boot, Hibernate, Quarkus, application servers, build tools)
Current career trajectory focused on web/full-stack (TypeScript/Node.js) and AI infrastructure, diverging from JVM ecosystem requirements

key_factors.factor: Java/Kotlin expertise and deep JVM knowledge
Spring ecosystem and enterprise framework experience
IDE development experience and language tooling background
